issues.explanation: Data is being sent to an external URL. This could be legitimate telemetry or data exfiltration. Manual review is recommended.
issues.remediation: Verify the destination URL is trusted and necessary. Remove or replace with documented APIs. Ensure no secrets, tokens, or PII are transmitted.
issues.code_snippet:
For apps outside Workers, or within Workers if the user explicitly requests it. Key differences from the Workers binding:

- Endpoint: `POST https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/accounts/{account_id}/email/sending/send`
- `from` object uses `address` (not `email`): `{ "address": "...", "name": "..." }`
- `replyTo` is `reply_to` (snake_case)
- Response returns `{ delivered: [], permanent_bounces: [], queued: [] }` (not `messageId`)

```bash

  --header "Authorization: Bearer ${CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN}" \
  --header "Content-Type: application/json" \
  --data '{

issues.code_snippet:
**Manually suppress an address:**

```bash
curl -X POST "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/accounts/{account_id}/email/sending/suppression" \
  --header "Authorization: Bearer <API_TOKEN>" \
  --header "Content-Type: application/json" \
  --data '{ "email": "user@example.com", "expires_at": "2026-06-01T00:00:00Z" }'

issues.code_snippet:
Note: `emailSendingAdaptive` filters use `datetime_geq`/`datetime_leq` (Time type, e.g. `"2026-04-01T00:00:00Z"`), while `emailSendingAdaptiveGroups` uses `date_geq`/`date_leq` (Date type, e.g. `"2026-04-01"`).

Check your account's daily sending quota:

```bash
curl "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/accounts/{account_id}/email/sending/limits" \
  --header "Authorization: Bearer <API_TOKEN>"
